snippet: Requirements for forming and running parents’ clubs are set out in the Education and Training Reform Regulations 2017 (Vic) (Regulations) and department policy. If there is no existing parents’ club at the school, and there is sufficient interest from a number of parents in forming a club, one or two interested parents should arrange to meet with the principal to discuss the process of formally establishing a new parents’ club. Principals and other school staff must not discourage parents from forming a parents’ club. The formal process is outlined in the section below. If the meeting outcome is a decision to start a parents’ club, the next steps are: seek the minister’s approval for the formation of the parents’ club under the terms of that constitution. The interim committee may not meet for any other purpose or conduct any other business including fundraising activities. Parents’ clubs that are not constituted in accordance with the Regulations will not be recognised by the school or the department.
